latest.title: Conversion of B Shares
latest.content:
12 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 3034W
Tungsten West PLC
12 March 2026
12 March 2026
Tungsten West Plc
("Tungsten West" or the "Company")
Conversion of B Shares
Tungsten West (AIM: TUN), the mining company focused on restarting production at the Hemerdon tungsten and tin mine in Devon in the UK, announces that on 6 and 11 March 2026, the Company received notices for the partial conversion of B Shares into new Ordinary Shares. The Company has received the following conversion notices from:
·
Drakewood Capital Management Limited to convert 10,339,891 B Shares into new Ordinary Shares on a one-to-one basis; and
·
Henry Maxey to convert 23,466,395 B Shares into new Ordinary Shares on a one-to-one basis.
Accordingly, the Company will issue an aggregate of 33,806,286 new Ordinary Shares on a one-to-one basis. Following the conversion, the number of outstanding B Shares not yet converted into Ordinary Shares will be 257,075,833.
Admission and Total Voting Rights
Applications have been made for the new Ordinary Shares to be admitted to trading on AIM ("Admission"). Admission is expected to become effective on 17 March 2026.
Immediately following Admission, the issued ordinary share capital of the Company is expected to comprise 1,247,672,923 Ordinary Shares and 257,075,833 B shares. Each Ordinary Share has one voting right and no Ordinary Shares are held in treasury. From Admission, this figure may be used by Shareholders as the denominator for the calculation by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Ends
For further information, please contact:
Enquiries
Tungsten West
Jeff Court, Chief Executive Officer
Phil Povey, Chief Financial Officer
Tel: +44 (0) 1752 278500

(Nominated Adviser and Financial Adviser)

company_30s.what_they_do: TUN.L is a small‑cap tungsten producer that mines, processes, and sells tungsten concentrates and alloys to manufacturers of hard‑metal tools, aerospace components, and high‑performance electronics.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Tungsten is a critical metal for cutting tools, jet engine parts, and advanced electronics. Demand is tightening as the industry moves toward lighter, stronger materials and the electric‑vehicle boom pushes up the need for high‑performance bearings and heat‑resistant alloys.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in the development phase, juggling several financing rounds and project milestones. It has not yet generated revenue from its flagship mine and is relying on capital raises to fund drilling and permitting work. The share price sits well below book value, reflecting the high risk and long development timeline.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy with capital‑raising activity—new subscriptions, a bookbuild, and a convertible loan conversion—showing that investors are still willing to put money into its projects.

Corporate governance updates (director dealings, share conversions) suggest the board is actively managing ownership structure, likely to keep the company afloat while it seeks a first‑sale or joint‑venture deal.
A recent project update indicates progress on drilling and environmental approvals, hinting at a potential milestone that could unlock future revenue streams.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet anticipation – the community is largely silent, but a handful of traders are quietly monitoring for a catalyst.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter focuses on the new funding round and the possibility that the mine could reach production in the next 12–18 months, which would dramatically lift the share price.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the company’s high debt load, lack of current cash flow, and the risk that the project might stall or be abandoned if financing dries up.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has been flat, but recent posts about a successful bookbuild have nudged sentiment slightly upward, giving a hint that a catalyst might be on the horizon.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat, meaning the market has not yet reacted to any news. A sudden announcement could trigger a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the next drilling phase and positive assay results could signal a move toward commercial production.
A new supply contract or partnership with a major OEM would provide a credible revenue stream.
A successful follow‑up funding round or a convertible note conversion that strengthens the balance sheet could remove a key risk factor.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that breaks the 50‑day moving average and a price bounce off the key support level around the 1‑month low. Any announcement of a first‑sale agreement or a regulatory approval would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a post‑crash recovery. A clear catalyst is still needed to move beyond the capitulation phase; until then, the upside is theoretical.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the share price climbs to just half of its pre‑crash level, the upside is roughly 200% or more, while the downside is capped at the current price. The deep discount combined with a potential first‑sale or funding event creates a classic asymmetric payoff.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company’s lack of revenue and heavy reliance on external financing mean that a failure to secure additional capital or a negative assay could push the price back to the bottom. The worst case is a further decline to a price that is effectively zero, but this is limited to the current market value.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk: potential loss of the entire investment (current price). Reward: upside of 2‑3× if the company reaches a modest production milestone. Risk‑reward ratio is roughly 1:3.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price closes above the 50‑day moving average and volume exceeds the 20‑day average. Use a stop 20% below the entry to protect against a false breakout. Allocate no more than 10% of the portfolio to this position.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash or a short position until you see a volume spike that breaks the 50‑day average or until a news item confirms a first‑sale agreement or new funding.
playbook.if_passing: None identified. The company has not shown any imminent delisting or bankruptcy signals, so a cautious bullish stance remains justified.

ai_insights.bottom_line: TUN.L sits at a deep discount with no current sentiment, but a forthcoming project milestone or funding round could trigger a sharp bounce, offering high upside with a limited downside.
